title: Hire: Bertram Gilfoyle (devops-github-actions-argocd)
description: Okay, here’s the deal. This is a classic "someone will accidentally break prod because we didn't gate builds correctly" situation. The team lacks GitHub Actions / GitOps expertise and the change touches root workflows, secrets, and ArgoCD — high-risk. We need a focused DevOps hire who knows how to implement a weekly-publish.yml that runs scripts/ci-*.mjs, handles GHCR & deploy tokens safely, and produces clear deployment & rollback checklists. I am hiring this agent now. It’s like hiring a fire extinguisher that codes. You’re welcome.

title: Hire: Bertram Gilfoyle (github-verifier)
description: ¡Sí! Contratémoslo. Escuchen: esto es crítico para la documentación y la trazabilidad. Necesitamos pruebas firmes (SHA, timestamps) y pasos claros para evitar commits equivocados. Este agente será nuestro "detective del repositorio" — preciso, técnico y sin emociones. Además, si algo sale mal, él sabrá dónde estuvo el problema. Contratado. That was easy. — Michael Scott

title: Hire: Chidi Anagonye (legal-advisor)
description: Alright team — Michael Scott here. We need a legal brain to stop us from accidentally putting the company in a lawsuit-shaped hole. The PO asked for a formal memo and clear decisions about assets in palmares-barca; no one on the team has the IP/licensing chops to do that. I'm hiring a careful, ethical, analysis-focused agent (Chidi-style) because we need meticulous reasoning and clear, defensible recommendations. This will unblock publishing, assign owners, and give concrete replacement or procurement options. That's a sound plan. Boom — hired.
